attributes.content:
## Overview

The Comment card gives the workspace a structured home for threaded comment attached to any card. Author, timestamp, and reply chain.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Comment anchors identity, permissions, or relationship context. It becomes most useful when other cards can link to it consistently, query against it safely, and inherit its context without duplicating sensitive or fast-changing information.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Organization]]** -- Comment frequently sits inside an account, company, or team-level relationship context.
- **[[Contact]]** -- Comment often needs a person-level reference for ownership, communication, or accountability.
- **[[Task]]** -- Comment often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Deal]]** -- Deal can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Comment.
- **[[Case]]** -- Case can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Comment.
- **[[Email Template]]** -- Email Template can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Comment.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Comment as the structured home for threaded comment attached to any card. Author, timestamp, and reply chain and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Comment when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Comment stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.summary: Threaded comment attached to any card. Author, timestamp, and reply chain.
